channel.title: SEAtoday - Food & Drink
channel.description: Upcoming events for SEAtoday - Food & Drink

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

Event details

item.title: Lynnwood Chamber Luncheon
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Location: Join our March Networking Luncheon, at Fogo de Chao with Special Guests, Lynnwood, WA

Description Sponsored by

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

Event details

item.title: Lunch Ladies
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Location: Changes Monthly, 319 4th Ave, Kirkland, WA

Join the ladies in our church every 3rd Thursday of the month for lunch and fellowship! Location rotates, RSVP and check the info center or app for location! Please RSVP so we know how big of a table to reserve!

Event details

item.title: FREE OFFICE AND COFFEE FRIDAYS!
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Spaces- Stadium, 450 Alaskan Way South #Suite 200, Seattle, WA

Join us for the day and treat yourself to one of our cozy, furnished office spaces. The coffee's on us!

Event details

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

Event details

item.title: Forterra Fridays
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Crestview Park, Seattle, WA

Forterra Fridays: March 2026 Friday, Mar. 20, 2026, 1:00 pm to 3:30 pm We'reexcited to invite you to volunteer with us at Crestview Park, a beloved community gathering space in Tukwila featuring dog play areas, trails, and natural landscapes. Join us on March 20, from 1:00-3:30 PM, to restore essential habitat by removing ivy and Himalayan blackberries that threaten our local native trees, shrubs, and groundcover. Established native plantsbenefitlocal wildlife, improve water quality, and enhance air quality. You will also gain valuable insights into ecological restoration and our innovative approach to land conservation.We'llstart with a walk of the site and have restoration professionals on hand to answer questions about plants, restoration, and habitat management. What we are doing: Crestview Park provides critical habitat restoration opportunities alongside its popular amenities. By removing invasive ivy and blackberries, we ensure that native plants can thrive, supporting the park's ecological health while maintaining the community spaces that make this park so valued by Tukwila residents. What to bring: Please dress for the weather \(in layers to stay warm/dry\). Snacks, drinks, tools, and gloves will all be provided. Where to park: Park in the Crestview Park parking lot \( 16200 42nd Avenue South\). Signs direct you from the parking lot to where we will meet. Date and Time: Friday, March 20, 2026, 1:00 -3:30 PM Register and learn more here .

Event details

item.title: The Taste of Iceland Festival March 19-21
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: National Nordic Museum, 2655 NW Market Street, Seattle, WA

Taste of Iceland returns to Seattle from Thursday, March 19, through Saturday, March 21. The cultural festival celebrates Iceland with events across the city showcasing the best of Icelandic culture, including food, music, nature, art, travel, and more. Most events are free and open to the public, including two free concerts, a prix fixe Icelandic dining experience, a comedy show, and a cocktail class. Tickets are required and guests are encouraged to arrive promptly to guarantee entry. Tickets are available on the Taste of Iceland Seattle website.  Taste of Iceland events include: Thursday, March 19 - Saturday, March 21 Icelandic Menu at Palace Kitchen: Ready to taste a plate full of Iceland? Icelandic Chef Ísak Aron Jóhannsson and Palace Kitchen Chef Ron Anderson will prepare a feast inspired by Iceland’s natural beauty and local flavors, including cod from Icelandic Seafood and lamb from Icelandic Lamb. This pop-up dining experience is available each evening beginning at 5:00 PM at Palace Kitchen. Reservations for the prix fixe menu are available at https://www.inspiredbyiceland.com/taste-of-iceland/seattle/icelandic-menu. Wheel of Prizes Presented by Icelandair: Select guests will have the opportunity to spin the Wheel of Prizes for a chance to win amazing prizes from all festival partners during multiple events, including a trip for two to Iceland! Learn more at https://www.inspiredbyiceland.com/taste-of-iceland/seattle/wheel-of-prizes. Thursday, March 19 Reykjavík Has Its Own Soundtrack: For one night only, Icelandic multi-instrumentalist, producer, and musician DJ Hermigervill invites Seattle audiences to experience an intimate listening party at Shibuya HiFi. The evening explores the songs that capture what it means to live and grow up in Iceland’s vibrant capital. Hermigervill opens his personal archive for an exclusive journey through Reykjavík’s musical identity, sharing rare recordings and the stories behind them. Presented in the spirit of Reykjavík Music City, the event celebrates a capital where the city helps shape the sound. 7:30 - 9:00 PM at Shibuya HiFi. Learn more at https://www.inspiredbyiceland.com/taste-of-iceland/seattle/reykjavik-soundtrack. Friday, March 20 Across Iceland: A Guided Cocktail Journey: During this two-hour cocktail class, storyteller and Icelandic guide Ragnar Bragason will lead guests through some of Iceland’s most stunning regions, sharing tales from the road and the places where Iceland Hotel Collection by BERJAYA welcomes travelers. At each stop, Icelandic mixologist, Arnór Gísli, will teach guests to create a signature cocktail inspired by the region's landscape, mood, and character. Three destinations. Three drinks. One unforgettable trip. 4:00 - 6:00 PM at Life on Mars. Learn more at https://www.inspiredbyiceland.com/taste-of-iceland/seattle/cocktail-journey. Northern Lights in Seattle: Learn about Iceland’s fascinating northern lights—which are expected to shine even brighter in 2026 and 2027—with astronomy and science educator, Sævar "Astro-Sævar" Helgi Bragason, hosted by Icelandia. Sævar will explain why Iceland is the perfect place to view the August 12, 2026, solar eclipse and recommend his favorite tours in Iceland. Guests will also be invited to experience Iceland through virtual reality, with a focus on Icelandia's Northern Lights tours, Glacier Hikes, and ATV tours. 6:00 - 7:30 PM at the National Nordic Museum. Learn more at https://www.inspiredbyiceland.com/taste-of-iceland/seattle/northern-lights. Iceland Airwaves Off-Venue: Enjoy a free concert curated by Iceland Airwaves, an award-winning music festival in Reykjavík, featuring RAKEL and Spacestation, with DJ Hermigervill spinning Icelandic tunes between sets. RAKEL’s intimate, atmospheric music combines voice and emotion into something quietly powerful. Spacestation blends shimmering rock and textured synths, creating a cosmic energy that feels both nostalgic and fresh. DJ Hermigervill is a multi-instrumentalist, producer, composer, and DJ. Doors open at 7:00 PM at Conor Byrne Co-op. Learn more at https://www.inspiredbyiceland.com/taste-of-iceland/seattle/iceland-airwaves-off-venue. Saturday, March 21 Draw Iceland’s Stories! Family Workshop with Illustrator Rán Flygenring: Join award-winning Icelandic storyteller and illustrator Rán Flygenring for a playful, inspiring family art workshop where imagination meets Icelandic nature and history. In this workshop, Rán invites children and parents alike to explore storytelling through drawing, doodling, and other creative activities. Families will learn about Iceland’s powerful landscapes, trailblazing leaders, and creative spirit. All ages are welcome, and no prior art experience is required—just curiosity, imagination, and a sense of fun! 11:00 AM - 2:00 PM at the National Nordic Museum. Sign up at https://www.inspiredbyiceland.com/taste-of-iceland/seattle/draw-icelands-stories for a chance to win a signed edition of one of Rán's illustrated children’s books. Iceland Unfiltered: Get ready for an evening of laughter hosted by Icelandair! At this one-night-only comedy event, two Icelanders are flying to Seattle to reveal the quirky truths, cultural oddities, and straight-up strange realities of Iceland. Comedians and storytellers Ragnar Bragason and Bergur Ebbi will share hilarious personal stories, offer wildly unqualified advice, and roast what tourists get wrong…every single time. Each guest will receive a complimentary cocktail infused with local flavors and Blue Lagoon Iceland will provide every guest with a skincare gift. Expect laughter and at least five conversation-starters for your next dinner party. Spoiler: You've definitely been pronouncing Reykjavík wrong. 4:00 - 6:00 PM at The Rabbit Box. Learn more at https://www.inspiredbyiceland.com/taste-of-iceland/seattle/iceland-unfiltered. Taste of Iceland was created to celebrate and introduce the traditions and culture of Iceland to North America. For more information on Taste of Iceland, please visit https://www.inspiredbyiceland.com/taste-of-iceland/seattle.

Event details

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

item.title: GirlBrunch
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: The Space, 3820 S Ferdinand Street 102, Seattle, WA

Brunch, music, and grown-woman energy. Join us for a curated daytime experience featuring a live DJ, elevated brunch selections \(vegans incl.\) and drinks! Coming with your girls? Perfect. Coming solo and looking to meet new friends? Also perfect. Girl Brunch is designed to feel welcoming,— whether you arrive with a group or by yourself. Dress up. Show up. Be open. Limited tickets available. If you have dietary needs, note that in your purchase before checkout! Gotta make sure you’re fed right girl!

Event details

item.title: La Dive Drag Brunch
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Location: La Dive, 721 East Pike Street, Seattle, WA

Join us at La Dive for Diamond Drag Brunch on March 22nd. We have a fierce and fabulous show for you featuring an amazing cast of Queens

Event details

item.title: Mimosas Cabaret ft. Death Becomes They/Them
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: The Mountain Room: Bar at the R, 3100 Airport Way South, Seattle, WA

Mimosas Cabaret serves up a fierce drag and burlesque brunch, at The Mountain Room, featuring "Death Becomes They/Them", a parody mini-musical packed with camp, glamour, and laughs. Sip cocktails while the stunning cast of Mimosas Cabaret deliver high energy performances inspired by the cult classic! Equal parts fabulous and unhinged, this brunch is your excuse to drink early, tip generously, and live forever! Siempre Viva!

Event details

item.title: The Grand Tasting [Taste Washington] - 2 Day Ticket
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: CenturyLink Field, 800 Occidental Ave S, Seattle, WA

Attention all food and wine lovers! Tickets for the highly anticipated Taste Washington Grand Tasting are now on sale!Don't miss your chance to experience the largest single-region wine and food festival in the country, featuring over 200 wineries, 70 restaurants, and a host of other exciting events and activities.\*Please note\* - One ticket is valid for both days.This event is ages 21\+ with valid ID. Please note vertical IDs are not accepted. Patrons must have a valid, horizontal ID. If you do not have a horizontal ID, a passport can be used as a substitute. There are no refunds or exchanges -- all sales are final.

Event details

item.title: Tractor Tavern Square Dance w/ Atlas String Band
item.description:
Monday, March 23, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Tractor Tavern, 5213 Ballard Ave NW, Seattle, WA

Traditional Square Dance night with an instructor who teaches you how to swing your partner to and fro. All skill levels welcome! No partner required!

Event details

item.title: TAF Varsity Luncheon 2026: Courage to Act
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Amazon Meeting Center (SEA45), 2031 7th Avenue, Seattle, WA

Join us on March 24, 2026 for this one-of-a-kind fundraising event!

Event details

item.title: Hyper-Local Happy Hour (Non-alcoholic)
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Calvary: The Hill, 1107 E Howell St, Seattle, WA

Get to know your hyper-local Capitol Hill neighbors around Cal Anderson Park in an open house style, non-alcoholic happy hour!  Enjoy non-alcoholic beers and yummy mocktails, along with a charcuterie spread, all inside a 120-year-old historic Capitol Hill church building. These happy hours will be from 4-5:30pm every 4th Tuesday through June  January 27th February 24th March 24th April 28th May 26th June 23rd \*Please note that ADA access is available into the happy hour area, but not to the restrooms unfortunately \(very old building!\)

Event details

item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

item.title: Spring in Full Bloom! A Brunch Celebration
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Location: University House Issaquah, 22975 SE Black Nugget Rd, Issaquah, WA

Join us for a delightful Spring Brunch as we celebrate the arrival of the season at University House Issaquah. Enjoy a relaxed gathering featuring a full brunch buffet, festive mimosas, and the fresh energy of spring. It’s the perfect opportunity to savor great food, connect with friends, and experience the welcoming atmosphere of our community. We hope you’ll join us for this cheerful celebration of new beginnings.

Event details

item.title: HAPPY HOUR
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Description FREE Event for Lynnwood Chamber Members & their Guests - please register in advance.

Event details

item.title: Women's Month Luncheon
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Location: Regency Newcastle, 7454 Newcastle Golf Club Rd, Newcastle, WA

Event details

item.title: Mariners Opening Day Beer Release with Fast Fashion
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 3 PM

Location: Chuck's Hop Shop Greenwood, 656 NW 85th St, Seattle, WA

Join us to celebrate the start of the Mariners season! We often visit Fast Fashion on game days, so it only made sense to brew up a beer to kick off another wonderful season! In addition to our West Coast IPA collab Heaven Sent, we'll have two other collabs on tap that Fast Fashion brewed with Structures and Formula!

Event details

item.title: FREE OFFICE AND COFFEE FRIDAYS!
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Spaces- Stadium, 450 Alaskan Way South #Suite 200, Seattle, WA

Join us for the day and treat yourself to one of our cozy, furnished office spaces. The coffee's on us!

Event details

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

Event details

item.title: Best of CID Food Tour
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Wing Luke Museum, 719 S. King Street, Seattle, WA

Best of CID Food Tour Friday, March 27 | 4:00PM-6:30PM General admission to the Museum is included with your tour ticket.

Event details

item.title: Live Music Happy Hour Joe Casalini Trio
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: The Royal Room, 5000 Rainier Ave South, Seattle, WA

Join us for Live Music Happy Hour with the Joe Casalini Trio. The Casalini Trio has played happy hours at the Royal Room since 2019. They play laid-back, swinging versions of great tunes by Duke Ellington, Billy Strayhorn, Hoagie Carmichael, Phillip Johnston, and other great American songwriters of the last 100 years. The happy hour usually lasts about an hour and a half. Good drinks, good people, good ambience, and there’s never a cover charge, although the band often encourages donations to local food banks and other worthy causes.

Event details

item.title: Making Local Markets
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 10 AM

Making Local Markets Downtown Bothell comes alive with creativity and community at the Making Local Markets on Saturday, March 28, 2026, from 10 AM to 4 PM. This vibrant outdoor market showcases 100\+ small, local, handmade businesses, plus an array of delicious food trucks. Stroll through the bustling streets, discover unique finds, and soak in the lively atmosphere. With pet-friendly vibes, free parking at City Hall, and plenty of great bars and restaurants nearby, it's the perfect way to spend a spring Saturday. Don't miss this celebration of local craftsmanship in the heart of Bothell!

Event details

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

Event details

item.title: PCC Cooking Class - Savory Sichuan
item.description:
Sunday, March 29, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Columbia City PCC, 3610 S Edmunds St, Seattle, WA

Learn to make traditional Chinese dishes at home! In this cooking class, you’ll pick up techniques and tips for capturing the flavors of Sichuan cuisine. Get practice infusing oil with chilis and hand-grinding spices for a sauce as you create deeply flavorful dan dan noodles. You’ll also try your hand at methodically rolling and flattening dough with scallions for savory pancakes, which you’ll pair with a scratch-made dipping sauce. By the end of class, you’ll be ready to recreate the entire menu in your kitchen.  Contains pork and dairy.  About the instructor: Our seasoned instructors and chefs have been chosen to be a part of PCC Cooks because of their culinary expertise and sincere enthusiasm and talent for teaching.  This is a hands-on format class for adults.  This cooking class is available at multiple PCC locations and dates. See below for details: Bellevue PCC -Friday, March 27, 6:00 - 8:00 pm  -Sunday, March 29, 10:00 - 12:00 pm  -Friday, April 3, 10:00 - 12:00 pm  Bothell PCC -Friday, March 27, 6:00 - 8:00 pm  -Sunday, March 29, 10:00 - 12:00 pm  Burien PCC -Sunday, March 29, 10:00 - 12:00 pm  Columbia City PCC -Sunday, March 29, 10:00 - 12:00 pm  Edmonds PCC -Friday, March 27, 6:00 - 8:00 pm  -Sunday, March 29, 10:00 - 12:00 pm  Green Lake Village PCC -Friday, March 27, 6:00 - 8:00 pm  -Sunday, March 29, 10:00 - 12:00 pm

Event details

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Sunday, March 29, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Event details

item.title: Mimosas Cabaret ft. Death Becomes They/Them
item.description:
Sunday, March 29, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: The Mountain Room: Bar at the R, 3100 Airport Way South, Seattle, WA

Mimosas Cabaret serves up a fierce drag and burlesque brunch, at The Mountain Room, featuring "Death Becomes They/Them", a parody mini-musical packed with camp, glamour, and laughs. Sip cocktails while the stunning cast of Mimosas Cabaret deliver high energy performances inspired by the cult classic! Equal parts fabulous and unhinged, this brunch is your excuse to drink early, tip generously, and live forever! Siempre Viva!

Event details

item.title: Paint & Sip at Post Pike Georgetown
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Post Pike Georgetown, 5701 Airport Way South, Seattle, WA

Come enjoy some drinks and pizza while painting step-by-step with me.

Event details

item.title: Tokyo Tea Room
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Barboza, 925 E. Pike Street, Seattle, WA

item.title: FloorWERK with Ruby with Ruby Mimosa
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Whim W'Him Contemporary Dance Center, 1716 2nd Avenue North, Seattle, WA

Find your inner diva on the floor with this sultry and sickening floor work class. You’ll have the audience gagged while screaming WEEEEERK!

Event details
